title: Quick CPU Capacity Estimator
description: Estimates CPU cores required based on request rate and service time
explanation: This pseudocode calculates CPU cores by multiplying requests per second by CPU time per request, then applying a safety factor. The CEILING function ensures we provision whole cores.

title: Little's Law Calculator
description: Calculates any variable in Little's Law formula L = λ × W
code:
FUNCTION LittleLawCalculator(knownVar, knownValue1, knownValue2, findVar)
 // knownVar: 'L' or 'lambda' or 'W'
 // findVar: variable to calculate
 
 lambda = 0 // Arrival rate (requests per second)
 W = 0 // Average time in system (seconds)
 L = 0 // Average items in system
 
 SWITCH findVar
 CASE 'L':
 // Calculate average items
 lambda = knownValue1
 W = knownValue2
 L = lambda * W
 PRINT "Average items in system: " + L
 RETURN L
 
 CASE 'lambda':
 // Calculate arrival rate
 L = knownValue1
 W = knownValue2
 lambda = L / W
 PRINT "Arrival rate: " + lambda + " req/sec"
 RETURN lambda
 
 CASE 'W':
 // Calculate average time
 L = knownValue1
 lambda = knownValue2
 W = L / lambda
 PRINT "Average time in system: " + W + " seconds (" + (W*1000) + " ms)"
 RETURN W
 ENDSWITCH
END FUNCTION

// Example: Find L when lambda=1000 req/sec, W=0.050 sec
// Result: L = 50 items
explanation: This function can solve for any of the three variables in Little's Law given the other two. Useful for capacity planning to find concurrency, throughput, or latency requirements.

title: Utilization and Response Time Calculator
description: Calculates wait time based on utilization and service time
explanation: This calculator demonstrates how response time degrades exponentially as utilization approaches 80%. The formula shows why capacity planning must maintain utilization below 70-80%.

title: Storage Capacity Quick Estimator
description: Estimates storage requirements with growth and compression
explanation: This estimator accounts for user data growth over time, compression ratios, base storage, and capacity buffer. Essential for initial storage capacity planning.
